title: Houses for Rent in Lakewood, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Lakewood, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Lakewood, Ohio? See typical rent ranges, transit, and housing stock, plus how to tell us what you need in Greater Cleveland.
answerBox: Lakewood, Ohio is a dense Lake Erie suburb immediately west of Cleveland (ZIP 44107), known for early-1900s single- and two-family homes. Rents across Greater Cleveland typically run $700–$1,800/month. Tell us what you're looking for in Lakewood and we'll let you know what fits.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Lakewood, Ohio
body: Lakewood is a Lake Erie suburb directly west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods, bordered roughly by the lake to the north and I-90 running through the southern part of the city, with **ZIP code 44107** covering most of the community. It's one of the most densely populated suburbs in Cuyahoga County, and its housing stock reflects that: a mix of early-1900s Colonials, bungalows, and two-family (duplex) houses on tight lots, many within a few blocks of Detroit Avenue or Madison Avenue.

**Rent Finder Cleveland works with 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods and select Ohio suburbs, and Lakewood isn't part of our current selection.** If you're set on Lakewood specifically, a Lakewood-focused property manager or listing site will have more direct inventory. If your search is flexible on location, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedrooms, budget, and whether you hold a housing voucher — and we can point you to comparable options in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods that we do work with.

One thing to factor into any Lakewood-versus-Cleveland comparison: Lakewood's density means smaller lots and less off-street parking than you'd typically find in outer suburbs, but it also puts most addresses within a short walk of Detroit Avenue's retail corridor. If a similar duplex unit is available at a comparable price in a bordering Cleveland neighborhood, the main trade-offs usually come down to lot size, parking, and which specific block a listing sits on.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent in the Cleveland area?
body: Rent in Lakewood itself varies with its dense multi-family stock, but as a general Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the metro's median asking rent at **$1,250/month** overall, with 2-bedroom units around **$1,100** and 3-bedroom units around **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally-managed buildings, shows a higher metro average of **$1,564/month**. Actual Lakewood house rents will depend on the specific block, unit size, and condition.

Lakewood sits directly against Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods, so it's a short drive or bus ride into the city along Detroit Avenue or Madison Avenue. It's served by Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes running along Detroit Avenue and Clifton Boulevard into downtown, and I-90 clips the southern edge of the city for drivers heading toward downtown Cleveland or west toward Rocky River and Westlake. Lakewood Park runs along the Lake Erie shoreline at the north end of the city. Because Lakewood sits on the west side of Cuyahoga County, it also tends to see less lake-effect snowfall over a winter than east-side suburbs like Cleveland Heights or Euclid — a geographic pattern tied to distance from the lake's prevailing wind path, not a comment on any neighborhood's desirability.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on Detroit Ave and Clifton Blvd; I-90 access
**Location:** Immediately west of Cleveland's Cudell/Edgewater area, on Lake Erie
**Housing stock:** Early-1900s single- and two-family homes, dense lot layout

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher holders looking in Lakewood
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and acceptance depends on the individual owner or municipality rather than the City of Lakewood having its own blanket rule confirmed here — always confirm directly with a specific landlord. What we can say with certainty is about our own selection: **every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, though our current inventory is concentrated in Cleveland proper rather than Lakewood. For general voucher information, the [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the local Housing Choice Voucher program and accepts preliminary applications year-round. See our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for more on how the program works locally.

If Lakewood is one option among several, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we do work with inventory, including several near Lakewood's east border. You can also compare notes on nearby suburbs like [Rocky River](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-rocky-river-ohio/) and [Westlake](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-westlake-ohio/), or browse our full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to see what's covered.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with houses in Lakewood, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our available homes of 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods and a few Ohio suburbs outside Lakewood. If you're flexible on location, tell us what you need and we'll point you to comparable options we do work with.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Lakewood, Ohio?
faq.a: Lakewood-specific figures vary by block and unit, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a metro median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report shows higher averages for larger professionally-managed buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Lakewood, Ohio in?
faq.a: Lakewood is primarily ZIP code 44107, sitting on Lake Erie directly west of Cleveland's Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods.

faq.q: Do Lakewood landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current homes are concentrated in Cleveland rather than Lakewood. CMHA administers the local voucher program.

faq.q: What's the housing stock like in Lakewood?
faq.a: Lakewood is known for early-1900s Colonials, bungalows, and two-family duplex houses on tight lots, reflecting its status as one of the most densely populated suburbs in Cuyahoga County. Many homes sit within a few blocks of the Detroit Avenue or Madison Avenue commercial corridors.
